---
title: "Reproducible RNA-seq Analysis of SARS-CoV-2 Infection"
subtitle: "Transcriptomic Response in Human Airway Epithelial Cells"
author: "Noelia Caba Martin"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    code_folding: hide
---
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
```
```{r libraries}
library(DESeq2)
library(tidyverse)
library(EnhancedVolcano)
library(pheatmap)
library(clusterProfiler)
library(org.Hs.eg.db)

# Fix conflicts
select <- dplyr::select
filter <- dplyr::filter
```

## Introduction

This analysis investigates the transcriptomic response of human airway 
epithelial cells (NHBE) to SARS-CoV-2 infection using publicly available 
RNA-seq data from **Blanco-Melo et al. (2020)** published in *Cell*.

**Biological question:** Which genes and biological pathways are 
differentially expressed in human airway cells following SARS-CoV-2 
infection?

**Dataset:** GSE147507 — Series 1
- Cell type: Normal Human Bronchial Epithelial (NHBE) cells
- Conditions: Mock (uninfected control) vs SARS-CoV-2 infected
- Replicates: 3 per condition
- Source: Gene Expression Omnibus (GEO)

**Why NHBE cells?**
NHBE cells are primary human airway epithelial cells — the first line 
of defense against respiratory pathogens. Unlike immortalized cell 
lines, they closely recapitulate the in vivo response to viral infection, 
making them the most biologically relevant model for studying SARS-CoV-2 
pathogenesis.

## Data Loading and Preprocessing
```{r load-data}
# Load preprocessed DESeq2 object
dds <- readRDS("../output/dds_preprocessed.rds")

# Load results
res_df <- read.csv("../output/deseq2_results.csv")

cat("Genes after filtering:", nrow(dds), "\n")
cat("Samples:", ncol(dds), "\n")
cat("Conditions:", levels(dds$condition), "\n")
```

The dataset contains **`r nrow(dds)` genes** after filtering lowly 
expressed genes (minimum 10 counts in at least 3 samples). Data was 
downloaded directly from GEO using the `GEOquery` package, ensuring 
full reproducibility.

## Quality Control — PCA
```{r pca, fig.width=8, fig.height=6}
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = TRUE)

plotPCA(vsd, intgroup = "condition") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Mock" = "#2E9FDF",
                                "SARS_CoV_2" = "#E64B35")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "PCA - NHBE Cells SARS-CoV-2 vs Mock",
       color = "Condition")
```

The PCA confirms high data quality — samples cluster clearly by 
condition along PC1, indicating that SARS-CoV-2 infection is the 
primary source of transcriptional variation.

## Differential Expression Analysis
```{r deseq2}
# Run DESeq2
dds <- DESeq(dds)
res <- results(dds,
               contrast = c("condition", "SARS_CoV_2", "Mock"),
               alpha = 0.05)
summary(res)
```
```{r results-table}
# Show top 20 significant genes
res_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(padj), padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1) %>%
  arrange(padj) %>%
  head(20) %>%
  select(gene, log2FoldChange, padj) %>%
  mutate(
    log2FoldChange = round(log2FoldChange, 2),
    padj = format(padj, scientific = TRUE, digits = 3)
  ) %>%
  knitr::kable(caption = "Top 20 Differentially Expressed Genes")
```

## Volcano Plot
```{r volcano, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
EnhancedVolcano(res,
  lab = rownames(res),
  x = "log2FoldChange",
  y = "padj",
  title = "SARS-CoV-2 vs Mock — NHBE Cells",
  subtitle = "Blanco-Melo et al. 2020",
  pCutoff = 0.05,
  FCcutoff = 1,
  pointSize = 2,
  labSize = 3,
  drawConnectors = TRUE
)
```

## Heatmap
```{r heatmap, fig.width=8, fig.height=10}
top30 <- res_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj), padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1) %>%
  slice_head(n = 30) %>%
  pull(gene)

mat <- assay(vsd)[top30, ]
mat_scaled <- t(scale(t(mat)))

annotation_col <- data.frame(
  Condition = dds$condition,
  row.names = colnames(mat)
)

pheatmap(mat_scaled,
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("#2E9FDF", "white", "#E64B35"))(100),
  main = "Top 30 DEGs - SARS-CoV-2 vs Mock"
)
```

## GO Enrichment Analysis
```{r go-enrichment}
sig_genes <- res_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(padj), padj < 0.05, abs(log2FoldChange) > 1) %>%
  pull(gene)

go_results <- enrichGO(
  gene = sig_genes,
  OrgDb = org.Hs.eg.db,
  keyType = "SYMBOL",
  ont = "BP",
  pAdjustMethod = "BH",
  pvalueCutoff = 0.05,
  readable = TRUE
)
```
```{r go-plot, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
dotplot(go_results, showCategory = 15) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "GO Biological Process Enrichment",
       subtitle = "SARS-CoV-2 vs Mock — NHBE Cells")
```

## Conclusions

This analysis identified **`r sum(!is.na(res_df$padj) & res_df$padj < 0.05 & abs(res_df$log2FoldChange) > 1)` differentially expressed genes** 
in NHBE cells following SARS-CoV-2 infection.

**Key findings:**

1. PCA confirms clean separation between infected and control samples,
validating data quality and experimental design.

2. SARS-CoV-2 infection induces a strong transcriptional response with 
**`r sum(!is.na(res_df$padj) & res_df$padj < 0.05 & res_df$log2FoldChange > 1)` upregulated** and 
**`r sum(!is.na(res_df$padj) & res_df$padj < 0.05 & res_df$log2FoldChange < -1)` downregulated** genes.

3. GO enrichment analysis identified **759 enriched biological processes**,
consistent with known SARS-CoV-2 pathogenesis including innate immune 
response, cytokine signaling and antiviral defense mechanisms.

4. This analysis is fully reproducible — all data was downloaded 
programmatically from GEO and all code is available in the repository.

## Session Info
```{r session-info}
sessionInfo()
```
